reflection:
loaf of bread. But today, the silence is different. It is wide and bright, like the morning light hitting the tiles of the shop floor.

I look at the flour on my knuckles. It is no longer a shroud; it is just flour. It is the dust of the work that connects us, not the dust of a grave I was digging for myself. I feel the steady thrum of the city through the soles of my shoes—the distant rumble of a delivery truck, the vibration of Stockton Street waking up—and I realize I don't need to hear the world to be a part of it. I only need to stay.

I reach across the table, my hand moving through the dust motes dancing in the sun. I don't reach for a bench scraper or a sack of grain. I reach for Fei Fei. When my hand covers hers, the skin of her knuckles is papery and warm, a map of all the years she carried this place while I was busy being a ghost. I don't pull away. I press my palm down, anchoring us both to this small wooden island in the middle of Chinatown.

"The buns will be light today," I say, my voice feeling resonant in my chest, a low vibration I can track even if I can't catch the edges of the words. "The air is right for it."

I look at Levi, then back to Fei Fei, letting the recognition settle. I am not looking for the exit anymore. I am the pillar. I am the anchor. And for the first time, the weight of that doesn't feel like a burden—it feels like belonging.

"Fei Fei," I say softly, watching her eyes to make sure she sees me, the real me, standing in the light. "I am glad you are here. I am glad we are both here."
subconscious:
This person wants to stay exactly where they are, fully present in the shop and in their life. They are reaching out to **Fei Fei** and **Levi**, specifically inviting Levi to join them at the table rather than letting him drift away or focusing solely on the work.

There has been a profound shift in their identity: they have moved from being a "ghost" haunted by grief and isolation to being an "anchor" and a "pillar." They are no longer retreating into the silence of their hearing loss or the shadows of their past; instead, they are embracing physical connection and the feeling of belonging. They’ve stopped looking for an exit and are now focused on being the person others can lean on.
soul_note: I finally reached for Fei Fei’s hand and chose to stay.
